summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the range of the correlation coefficient r?

The correlation coefficient r always falls between −1 and 1 inclusive.

What is 'correlation'?

Correlation measures how strongly and in what direction two variables are linearly associated, quantified by the correlation coefficient r.

What is the 'correlation coefficient' (r)?

r ranges from −1 (perfect negative) to 1 (perfect positive). r = 0 means no linear correlation.

What is 'linear regression'?

Linear regression finds the equation ŷ = mx + b of the line that best fits the data by minimizing the sum of squared residuals.

What is a 'residual' in regression?

A residual measures the vertical distance between an actual data point and the regression line's predicted value.

What is an 'outlier' in the context of regression?

An outlier is a data point that deviates significantly from the trend. It can disproportionately affect the slope and intercept of the regression line.

A regression line for predicting home prices (y, in $1000s) from square footage (x) is ŷ = 0.15x + 50. The actual price of a 2000 sq ft home is $380,000. What is the residual?

Predicted: ŷ = 0.15(2000) + 50 = 350 ($350,000). Residual = 380 − 350 = 30 ($30,000).

Ice cream sales and drowning incidents both increase in summer. A student concludes ice cream causes drowning. What is the flaw?

Hot weather is a confounding variable that increases both ice cream sales and swimming (and thus drowning risk). Correlation does not imply causation.

A company finds the regression equation for employee experience (years) and salary ($) is ŷ = 3,000x + 35,000. What is the predicted salary for 10 years of experience?

ŷ = 3,000(10) + 35,000 = 30,000 + 35,000 = $65,000.

A data set has r = −0.15. What is the best interpretation?

r = −0.15 is close to 0, indicating a very weak negative linear association between the variables.

summary_sections.title: Outliers and Resistant Measures
summary_sections.content_html:
Outliers and Resistant Measures

An outlier is a data point that deviates significantly from the trend. It can disproportionately affect the slope and intercept of the regression line.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: If r² = 0.81, what percentage of variation in y is explained by x?

r² = 0.81 means 81% of the variation in y is explained by the linear relationship with x.

Example: A study finds r = 0.92 between hours studied and test scores. A student claims studying more causes higher scores. Is this conclusion valid?

Even with r = 0.92, correlation alone cannot establish causation. An experiment with controls would be needed.

Example: A regression line for predicting home prices (y, in $1000s) from square footage (x) is ŷ = 0.15x + 50. The actual price of a 2000 sq ft home is $380,000. What is the residual?

Predicted: ŷ = 0.15(2000) + 50 = 350 ($350,000). Residual = 380 − 350 = 30 ($30,000).

Example: A coach tracks the number of practice hours (x) and game points scored (y). Data gives r² = 0.64. What does this mean?

r² = 0.64 means 64% of the variability in y is accounted for by the linear relationship with x. The remaining 36% is unexplained.

Example: A researcher uses the regression ŷ = 5x − 10 to predict y for x = 50, even though data was collected for x between 5 and 30. This is an example of:

x = 50 is outside the observed range of 5–30. Predicting there is extrapolation and the linear trend may not continue.

Example: Ice cream sales and drowning incidents both increase in summer. A student concludes ice cream causes drowning. What is the flaw?

Hot weather is a confounding variable that increases both ice cream sales and swimming (and thus drowning risk). Correlation does not imply causation.

Example: A student collects data on sleep hours (x) and quiz scores (y) for 20 students. The regression equation is ŷ = 5x + 40 with r = 0.78. Based on the model, what score is expected for a student who sleeps 8 hours?

ŷ = 5(8) + 40 = 40 + 40 = 80. With r = 0.78, the model explains a good portion of variability.
